contents.value: **MATCH** — Cypher keyword.
**WHERE** — Cypher keyword.
**RETURN** — Cypher keyword.
**CREATE** — Cypher keyword.
**DELETE** — Cypher keyword.
**count** — Built-in function/command in Cypher.
**sum** — Built-in function/command in Cypher.
**avg** — Built-in function/command in Cypher.
**min** — Built-in function/command in Cypher.
**max** — Built-in function/command in Cypher.

```sql
SELECT column1, column2 FROM table WHERE condition
```
**SELECT** retrieves data from one or more tables.

```sql
INSERT INTO table (col1, col2) VALUES (val1, val2)
```
**INSERT** adds new rows to a table.

```sql
UPDATE table SET col = val WHERE condition
```
**UPDATE** modifies existing rows.

```sql
SELECT * FROM a JOIN b ON a.id = b.id
```
**JOIN** combines rows from multiple tables based on a related column.

An **index** speeds up queries by creating an optimized lookup structure.
```sql
CREATE INDEX idx ON table(column);
```

A **view** is a virtual table defined by a query.
```sql
CREATE VIEW name AS SELECT ...;
```

A **transaction** groups operations that must succeed or fail as a unit.
```sql
BEGIN; ... COMMIT; -- or ROLLBACK;
```

**Aggregate functions** compute a single result from a set of rows.
```sql
COUNT(*), SUM(col), AVG(col), MIN(col), MAX(col)
```

A **subquery** is a query nested inside another query.
```sql
SELECT * FROM t WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM t2)
```

A **Common Table Expression** defines a named temporary result set.
```sql
WITH cte AS (SELECT ...) SELECT * FROM cte
```

**Constraints** enforce rules on data in tables.
```sql
PRIMARY KEY, FOREIGN KEY, UNIQUE, NOT NULL, CHECK
```
**NULL** represents the absence of a value. Use IS NULL / IS NOT NULL to test.

**Window functions** compute values across a set of rows related to the current row.
```sql
ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY col ORDER BY col)
```

**GROUP BY** groups rows with the same values for aggregate calculations.
```sql
SELECT dept, COUNT(*) FROM emp GROUP BY dept
```

```cypher
MERGE (n:Label {key: value}) [ON CREATE SET ...] [ON MATCH SET ...]
```
Ensures a pattern exists in the graph by either matching an existing element or creating a new one if no match is found. MERGE is an idempotent operation that prevents duplicate nodes and relationships, with optional ON CREATE and ON MATCH clauses for conditional property setting.

```cypher
SET n.property = value | SET n:Label | SET n += {props}
```
Updates properties on nodes and relationships or adds labels to nodes. SET can assign a single property, add a label, or merge a map of properties. Using += merges properties (preserving existing ones), while = replaces all properties.

```cypher
WITH expression [AS alias] [WHERE condition]
```
Chains query parts together by passing results from one part to the next, acting as both a projection and a pipeline separator. WITH allows intermediate filtering, aggregation, and variable scoping within a single Cypher query, similar to a subquery boundary.

```cypher
UNWIND list AS variable
```
Expands a list into individual rows, one per element. UNWIND is essential for processing collections passed as parameters, transforming list properties into rows for further matching or creation, and implementing batch operations in Cypher queries.

```cypher
String
```
Text data type representing a sequence of characters.

```cypher
Number
```
Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

```cypher
Boolean
```
Logical type with two values: true and false.

```cypher
Array
```
Ordered collection of elements accessible by index.

```cypher
Object
```
Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).

```cypher
null
```
Represents the intentional absence of any value.

```cypher
undefined
```
Represents an uninitialized or missing value.

```cypher
print(value)
```
Output a value to the standard output stream.

```cypher
length
```
Returns the number of elements in a collection or characters in a string.
